Hey there,

Welcome to your weekly roundup of Puck\xe2\x80\x99s Inner Circle Exclusive hits from the past few weeks.

In this issue: Lauren Sherman scoops Herm\xc3\xa8s\xe2\x80\x99s somewhat startling plan to expand into couture\xe2\x80\x94and offers an inside look at how the L.A. fires are impacting an already scrambled fashion show season. Meanwhile, Marion Maneker shares his t\xc3\xaate-\xc3\xa0-t\xc3\xaate with Rome-based private art dealer Mattia De Luca about the highly anticipated Giorgio Morandi exhibition at Zwirner, and pores over some proprietary data that signals where the art market is headed in 2025.

Enjoy!

Herm\xc3\xa8s Goes Up, Up, Up Market

LAUREN SHERMAN

The legendary, family-controlled leather goods house is contemplating an unprecedented and decidedly old-school move: couture. Do the costs and craft required for this rarefied market make sense for the already stellar quarter-trillion-dollar business?

The Morandi Moment

MARION MANEKER

Mattia De Luca\xe2\x80\x99s dream was to hold a pop-up exhibition of his favorite artist, Giorgio Morandi, in New York. On the eve of another important Morandi show at David Zwirner, he speaks about his experience with the artist.

The Fires, Fashion & The Red Carpet

LAUREN SHERMAN

The industry is anxiously waiting for Hollywood\xe2\x80\x99s A-list to RSVP to the upcoming shows in New York, Milan, Paris, and London\xe2\x80\x94and those anxieties have only been amplified by the fires. \xe2\x80\x9cThe big houses are worried,\xe2\x80\x9d one C-suite luxury executive told me. \xe2\x80\x9cWe know the celebrity impact.\xe2\x80\x9d

The Art Market in Numbers

MARION MANEKER

New, proprietary data shows a 25 percent drop in art sales last year across Christie\xe2\x80\x99s, Sotheby\xe2\x80\x99s, and Phillips\xe2\x80\x94a brutal signal, perhaps, that the market has truly hit bottom. Nevertheless, there are encouraging signs within certain market segments, such as more buyers bidding, and a growing number of lots selling above the estimate range. Herewith, an autopsy\xe2\x80\xa6
